 {"id":"bd-2zf","title":"Implement IndeterminateSpinner for unknown-length operations","description":"## Purpose
Create a spinner renderable for operations where we do not know the total count or duration, showing activity with elapsed time.

## Background
Many database operations have unknown duration:
- Establishing connections
- Running complex queries
- Waiting for locks
- Initial data discovery

Users need feedback that something is happening, even without a completion percentage.

## Implementation Details

### File Location
crates/sqlmodel-console/src/renderables/spinner.rs

### Core Struct
IndeterminateSpinner holds:
- message: String
- started_at: Instant
- style: SpinnerStyle enum
- theme: Theme

### SpinnerStyle Enum
- Dots: ... (three dots cycling)
- Braille: Unicode braille pattern animation
- Line: -/|\ rotating
- Arrow: arrow rotating
- Simple: asterisk blinking

### Frame Generation
Each style defines frames array and interval_ms.
Example Dots: [".", "..", "...", ".."] at 250ms intervals

### Rendering (Rich Mode)
[...] Connecting to database (2.3s)
^---- spinner animation
     ^---- message
                           ^---- elapsed time

### Plain Text (Agent Mode)
[...] Connecting to database (2.3s)
(Static output, no animation - agents see single line)

## API Design
IndeterminateSpinner::new(message)
  .style(SpinnerStyle::Dots)
  .theme(theme)
  .render(width) / .render_plain()

### Convert to Progress
When length becomes known, convert spinner to progress:
let progress = spinner.into_progress(total);

### Live Update Pattern
let spinner = IndeterminateSpinner::new("Connecting");
console.start_spinner(&spinner);
// ... do work
console.stop_spinner(&spinner);

## Verification Steps
1. Test each spinner style
2. Verify elapsed time formatting (s, m:ss)
3. Test conversion to progress bar
4. Verify plain text output
5. Test with long messages (truncation)
6. Verify animation timing

## Dependencies
- Theme from this crate
- std::time::Instant for timing","acceptance_criteria":"IndeterminateSpinner shows activity animation
Spinner has multiple style options
Spinner respects terminal width
Plain mode outputs dots or status text
All unit tests verify animation frames
Performance tests confirm low overhead","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:12:38.251317294Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T11:25:34.436765300Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T11:25:34.436722440Z","close_reason":"Implemented IndeterminateSpinner with 5 styles, conversion to progress bar, and 20 unit tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2zf","depends_on_id":"bd-1q2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2zf","depends_on_id":"bd-1rn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":17,"issue_id":"bd-2zf","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Unit Tests to Implement

1. test_spinner_creation - verify initial state
2. test_spinner_all_styles - verify each SpinnerStyle
3. test_spinner_frame_generation - verify animation frames
4. test_spinner_elapsed_time - verify duration tracking
5. test_spinner_render_plain - verify text output
6. test_spinner_message_update - verify message changes
7. test_spinner_convert_to_progress - verify conversion
8. test_spinner_render_rich - verify rich output (with feature)","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:27:07Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-318","title":"Phase 8: Facade Crate Integration and Public API","description":"## Purpose
Integrate the console system into the main sqlmodel facade crate, providing a unified public API for console configuration and usage across all sqlmodel components.

## Background
The sqlmodel facade crate is the main entry point for users. It must:
- Re-export console types and traits
- Provide easy console configuration
- Integrate console with high-level operations
- Maintain backward compatibility for users not using console

## Key Deliverables

### 1. Console Re-exports in sqlmodel Prelude
Add to prelude:
- SqlModelConsole
- OutputMode
- Theme
- ConsoleAware trait
- Key renderables (ErrorPanel, QueryResultTable, etc.)

### 2. Console Builder Integration
Extend existing builders to accept console:
Session::builder()
    .with_console(console)
    .connect(url)

### 3. Global Console Support
Optional global console for convenience:
sqlmodel::set_global_console(console);
// Now all operations use this console automatically

### 4. Configuration API
Easy configuration without deep imports:
use sqlmodel::prelude::*;

let console = SqlModelConsole::builder()
    .auto_detect_mode()  // agent vs human
    .theme(Theme::dark())
    .build();

### 5. Feature Flag Coordination
Ensure console feature properly propagates:
- sqlmodel enables sqlmodel-console when console feature active
- Drivers enable their console support when console active
- All is optional, no bloat for non-console users

## Integration Points
- Session/connection builders
- Query execution methods
- Error handling paths
- Schema operations

## Agent Safety Preserved
The facade maintains agent safety guarantees:
- Default is auto-detect mode
- Plain mode always available
- No breaking changes to stdout semantics

## Dependencies
- Phase 2-7 complete (all console infrastructure ready)
- All driver integrations complete

## Verification
- Test prelude imports work
- Test builder integration
- Test global console pattern
- Verify feature flags work correctly","acceptance_criteria":"sqlmodel prelude re-exports console types when feature enabled
Session and Connection builders accept console configuration
Global console support works for convenience usage
Feature-gated compilation works correctly
All unit tests pass for facade integration
Documentation shows correct usage patterns","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:14:06.150467154Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T21:45:52.471189789Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T21:45:52.471146628Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-318","depends_on_id":"bd-88i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-318","depends_on_id":"bd-eqb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":18,"issue_id":"bd-318","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Console types exported in sqlmodel prelude
- [ ] Session::builder().with_console() works
- [ ] Global console via sqlmodel::set_global_console()
- [ ] Console propagates from session to connection
- [ ] Feature flag 'console' properly gates all functionality
- [ ] No compile errors without console feature
- [ ] Integration tests verify API surface","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:37:34Z"},{"id":19,"issue_id":"bd-318","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Fixed thread-safety issue in global console by removing the rich_rust::Console field from SqlModelConsole (which contained Cell/RefCell types that are not Sync). The rich console functionality is still available but will be created on-demand rather than stored. All tests pass.","created_at":"2026-01-21T21:45:36Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3gj","title":"Implement OperationProgress bar for determinate operations","description":"## Purpose
Create a progress bar renderable for operations where we know the total count, showing completion percentage, throughput, and ETA.

## Background
Bulk operations (batch inserts, data exports, migrations) need progress feedback. Users want to see:
- How much is done vs remaining
- Current throughput
- Estimated time to completion
- Running elapsed time

## Implementation Details

### File Location
crates/sqlmodel-console/src/renderables/operation_progress.rs

### Core Struct
OperationProgress holds:
- completed: u64
- total: u64
- started_at: Instant
- operation_name: String
- theme: Theme
- width: Option<usize>
- show_eta: bool
- show_throughput: bool

### Rendering (Rich Mode)
Uses rich_rust ProgressBar with customizations:
- Colored bar based on completion percentage
- Percentage display
- Items processed counter
- Throughput calculation
- ETA based on current rate

Format: Inserting rows [===>    ] 42% (420/1000) 50.2/s ETA: 12s

### Throughput Calculation
rate = completed / elapsed_seconds
eta_seconds = (total - completed) / rate

### Style Variations
- Standard: Blue bar
- Success (100%): Green bar
- Warning (slow): Yellow bar
- Error (stalled): Red bar

### Plain Text (Agent Mode)
Inserting rows: 42% (420/1000) 50.2 rows/s ETA: 12s

## API Design
OperationProgress::new(name, total)
  .completed(n)  // or .increment() / .add(n)
  .theme(theme)
  .show_eta(true)
  .show_throughput(true)
  .width(60)
  .render(width) / .render_plain()

### Update Pattern (for live progress)
let mut progress = OperationProgress::new("Inserting", 1000);
for batch in batches {
    // do work
    progress.add(batch.len());
    console.update_progress(&progress);
}

## Verification Steps
1. Test at 0%, 50%, 100%
2. Verify throughput calculation accuracy
3. Verify ETA reasonableness
4. Test with very fast operations (sub-second)
5. Test with very slow operations (minutes)
6. Verify plain text parseable format
7. Test width constraints

## Dependencies
- rich_rust ProgressBar component
- Theme from this crate
- std::time::Instant for timing","acceptance_criteria":"OperationProgress shows percentage completion
Progress bar respects terminal width
Progress shows elapsed time and ETA
Plain mode outputs percentage updates
All unit tests verify progress calculation
Performance tests confirm low overhead","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:12:25.180736054Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T11:21:11.842918701Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T11:21:11.842865300Z","close_reason":"Implemented OperationProgress with throughput, ETA, and 25+ unit tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3gj","depends_on_id":"bd-1q2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3gj","depends_on_id":"bd-1rn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":20,"issue_id":"bd-3gj","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Unit Tests to Implement

1. test_progress_creation - verify initial state
2. test_progress_percentage_calculation - 0%, 50%, 100%
3. test_progress_increment - verify counter updates
4. test_progress_add_batch - verify batch additions
5. test_progress_throughput - verify rate calculation
6. test_progress_eta_estimation - verify ETA accuracy
7. test_progress_render_plain - verify text format
8. test_progress_render_rich - verify segments (with feature)
9. test_progress_zero_total - edge case handling
10. test_progress_rate_smoothing - verify stable rates
11. test_progress_very_fast_operations - sub-ms timing
12. test_progress_very_slow_operations - multi-minute

Include timing simulation helpers.","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:26:58Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3j0","title":"Add sqlmodel-console to workspace Cargo.toml","description":"# Add sqlmodel-console to Workspace Cargo.toml

## Task Description

Register the new `sqlmodel-console` crate in the workspace root Cargo.toml so it's
recognized as part of the sqlmodel_rust workspace.

## Changes Required

### In /data/projects/sqlmodel_rust/Cargo.toml

Add to the `[workspace]` members list:
```toml
[workspace]
members = [
    "crates/sqlmodel",
    "crates/sqlmodel-core",
    "crates/sqlmodel-macros",
    "crates/sqlmodel-query",
    "crates/sqlmodel-schema",
    "crates/sqlmodel-pool",
    "crates/sqlmodel-postgres",
    "crates/sqlmodel-sqlite",
    "crates/sqlmodel-mysql",
    "crates/sqlmodel-console",  # ADD THIS LINE
]
```

### Optional: Add to Workspace Dependencies

If we want other crates to depend on sqlmodel-console, add:
```toml
[workspace.dependencies]
sqlmodel-console = { path = "crates/sqlmodel-console" }
```

## Verification

```bash
# From workspace root
cargo check --workspace

# Specifically check the new crate
cargo check -p sqlmodel-console

# Verify it appears in workspace
cargo metadata --format-version=1 | jq '.workspace_members[] | select(contains("sqlmodel-console"))'
```

## Important Notes

- The workspace Cargo.toml is the source of truth for workspace membership
- Order in members list doesn't matter but alphabetical is nice
- Don't add features to workspace.dependencies yet; let each crate specify what it needs
- This task cannot be done until the crate directory and Cargo.toml exist

## Why This Matters

Without workspace registration:
- `cargo check --workspace` won't include the crate
- `cargo test --workspace` won't run its tests
- IDE tooling may not recognize it
- Other crates can't depend on it via workspace path","acceptance_criteria":"sqlmodel-console added to workspace members
Workspace Cargo.toml parses correctly
cargo check from workspace root includes console crate
All existing crates still build correctly","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:03:24.635072826Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T09:09:54.998040989Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T09:09:54.997102201Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["phase-1","rich-rust","workspace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3j0","depends_on_id":"bd-1vz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3j0","depends_on_id":"bd-tel","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-59d","title":"Create SchemaTree renderable for database structure visualization","description":"## Purpose
Implement a tree renderable that displays database schema structure in a hierarchical format, perfect for visualizing table relationships, columns, indexes, and constraints.

## Background
When users inspect database schemas, they need to see:
- Tables as tree nodes
- Columns with types and constraints as children
- Indexes and foreign keys as nested items
- Visual distinction between primary keys, nullable, indexed columns

This uses rich_rust's Tree component to create an intuitive visual representation.

## Implementation Details

### File Location
`crates/sqlmodel-console/src/renderables/schema_tree.rs`

### Core Struct
```rust
use rich_rust::prelude::*;
use sqlmodel_schema::{TableSchema, ColumnInfo, IndexInfo, ForeignKey};

/// Configuration for schema tree rendering
pub struct SchemaTreeConfig {
    /// Show column types inline
    pub show_types: bool,
    /// Show constraints (PK, FK, NOT NULL)
    pub show_constraints: bool,
    /// Show indexes
    pub show_indexes: bool,
    /// Color theme
    pub theme: Theme,
}

impl Default for SchemaTreeConfig {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            show_types: true,
            show_constraints: true,
            show_indexes: true,
            theme: Theme::default(),
        }
    }
}

/// Renders a database schema as a tree structure
pub struct SchemaTree<'a> {
    tables: &'a [TableSchema],
    config: SchemaTreeConfig,
    title: Option<String>,
}

impl<'a> SchemaTree<'a> {
    pub fn new(tables: &'a [TableSchema]) -> Self {
        Self {
            tables,
            config: SchemaTreeConfig::default(),
            title: None,
        }
    }

    pub fn with_config(mut self, config: SchemaTreeConfig) -> Self {
        self.config = config;
        self
    }

    pub fn title(mut self, title: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
        self.title = Some(title.into());
        self
    }

    /// Build tree node for a single table
    fn build_table_node(&self, table: &TableSchema) -> TreeNode {
        let table_label = format\!(
            "[{}]{}[/]",
            self.config.theme.table_name_color(),
            table.name
        );
        let mut node = TreeNode::new(&table_label);

        // Add columns
        for col in &table.columns {
            let col_node = self.build_column_node(col, table);
            node.add_child(col_node);
        }

        // Add indexes if enabled
        if self.config.show_indexes {
            for idx in &table.indexes {
                let idx_node = self.build_index_node(idx);
                node.add_child(idx_node);
            }
        }

        node
    }

    fn build_column_node(&self, col: &ColumnInfo, table: &TableSchema) -> TreeNode {
        let mut parts = vec\![col.name.clone()];

        if self.config.show_types {
            parts.push(format\!(
                "[{}]{}[/]",
                self.config.theme.type_color(),
                col.sql_type
            ));
        }

        if self.config.show_constraints {
            let mut constraints = Vec::new();
            if col.is_primary_key {
                constraints.push(format\!(
                    "[{}]PK[/]",
                    self.config.theme.primary_key_color()
                ));
            }
            if \!col.nullable {
                constraints.push(format\!(
                    "[{}]NOT NULL[/]",
                    self.config.theme.constraint_color()
                ));
            }
            if col.has_default {
                constraints.push(format\!(
                    "[{}]DEFAULT[/]",
                    self.config.theme.default_color()
                ));
            }
            if \!constraints.is_empty() {
                parts.push(constraints.join(" "));
            }
        }

        TreeNode::new(&parts.join(" "))
    }

    fn build_index_node(&self, idx: &IndexInfo) -> TreeNode {
        let idx_type = if idx.unique { "UNIQUE" } else { "INDEX" };
        let label = format\!(
            "[{}]{}[/] {} ({})",
            self.config.theme.index_color(),
            idx_type,
            idx.name,
            idx.columns.join(", ")
        );
        TreeNode::new(&label)
    }

    /// Render to segments for rich mode
    pub fn render(&self, width: usize) -> Vec<Segment> {
        let root_label = self.title.clone()
            .unwrap_or_else(|| "Database Schema".to_string());
        let mut root = TreeNode::new(&format\!(
            "[bold {}]{}[/]",
            self.config.theme.heading_color(),
            root_label
        ));

        for table in self.tables {
            root.add_child(self.build_table_node(table));
        }

        let tree = Tree::new(root);
        tree.render(width)
    }

    /// Render to plain text for agent mode
    pub fn render_plain(&self) -> String {
        let mut output = String::new();
        let title = self.title.as_deref().unwrap_or("Database Schema");
        output.push_str(&format\!("{}\n", title));
        output.push_str(&"=".repeat(title.len()));
        output.push(n);

        for table in self.tables {
            output.push_str(&format\!("\nTable: {}\n", table.name));
            for col in &table.columns {
                let mut line = format\!("  - {}: {}", col.name, col.sql_type);
                if col.is_primary_key {
                    line.push_str(" [PK]");
                }
                if \!col.nullable {
                    line.push_str(" NOT NULL");
                }
                output.push_str(&format\!("{}\n", line));
            }
        }
        output
    }
}
```

## Usage Example
```rust
let tables = vec\![user_schema, post_schema];
let tree = SchemaTree::new(&tables)
    .title("my_database")
    .with_config(SchemaTreeConfig {
        show_indexes: true,
        ..Default::default()
    });

console.print_renderable(&tree);
```

## Visual Output (Rich Mode)
```
📊 my_database
├── users
│   ├── id INTEGER PK NOT NULL
│   ├── name TEXT NOT NULL
│   ├── email TEXT NOT NULL
│   └── UNIQUE idx_users_email (email)
└── posts
    ├── id INTEGER PK NOT NULL
    ├── user_id INTEGER NOT NULL
    ├── title TEXT NOT NULL
    └── INDEX idx_posts_user (user_id)
```

## Verification Steps
1. Create test schema with multiple tables
2. Verify tree renders correctly in rich mode
3. Verify plain text output for agent mode
4. Test with various config options
5. Ensure colors match Theme settings
6. Test with empty schema (no tables)
7. Test with table with no indexes

## Dependencies
- sqlmodel-schema for TableSchema, ColumnInfo types
- rich_rust Tree and TreeNode components
- Theme from this crate","acceptance_criteria":"SchemaTree displays tables, columns, indexes hierarchically
Tree shows foreign key relationships
Tree supports filtering by table pattern
Plain mode outputs indented text representation
All unit tests verify tree construction
Integration tests verify schema parsing","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:10:09.907805830Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T06:59:13.567971119Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T06:59:13.567902722Z","close_reason":"Implemented - parent phase closed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-59d","depends_on_id":"bd-1rn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-59d","depends_on_id":"bd-2g8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":21,"issue_id":"bd-59d","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Unit Tests to Implement

1. test_schema_tree_creation - verify construction

3. test_schema_tree_multiple_tables - verify multi-table

8. test_schema_tree_render_rich - verify rich output
9. test_schema_tree_empty_schema - edge case
10. test_schema_tree_config_options - verify config flags

Use schema fixtures from bd-1pw.","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:27:17Z"}]}
